arel-03.reason: fixture returns a bare Arel::Attributes::Attribute (posts[:id]); Rails Arel::Attribute does not implement #to_sql — trails Attribute#toSql emits a quoted column ref. Fixture-design divergence; upstream Rails treats bare attributes as non-renderable.

arel-04.reason: fixture returns posts[Arel.star] (an Attribute); same as arel-03 — Rails Arel::Attribute#to_sql undefined, trails renders 'posts'.'*'.

arel-16.reason: does_not_match_regexp: Rails does not expose it on SQLite (regex operators are adapter-specific); trails ToSql visitor errors with 'NotRegexp is not supported by the base ToSql visitor'. Needs adapter-specific visitors on both sides.

arel-17.reason: is_distinct_from: trails emits 'IS DISTINCT FROM' (SQL standard); Rails on SQLite emits 'IS NOT' (SQLite extension). Both are semantically equivalent but lexically differ.

arel-24.reason: arithmetic chain (users[:age] / 3 - employees[:time_at_company]): trails Attribute#divide returns a node without a .subtract method — predication-after-arithmetic chain is incomplete.

arel-25.reason: arithmetic predication gap — same family as arel-24 (trails arithmetic ops do not chain into further predicates).

arel-26.reason: arithmetic predication gap — same family as arel-24.

arel-27.reason: arithmetic predication gap — same family as arel-24.

arel-28.reason: arithmetic predication gap — same family as arel-24.

arel-29.reason: arithmetic predication gap — same family as arel-24.

arel-30.reason: (~users[:bitmap]).gt(0): trails Nodes.BitwiseNot does not implement predication methods (no .gt/.lt/.eq chain).

arel-36.reason: EXTRACT(MONTH FROM ...) vs EXTRACT(month FROM ...): trails ToSql visitor emits the EXTRACT field in the casing it was constructed with (lowercase in fixture); Rails uppercases.

arel-44.reason: subquery alias quoting: trails wraps the alias in double quotes ('\'sub\''); Rails emits the alias bare (sub). Real ToSql visitor divergence.

arel-47.reason: photos[:id].count.gt(5): trails Attribute#count returns a node without predication methods — HAVING aggregate-comparison chain incomplete.

arel-55.reason: Arel::Attributes::Attribute#multiply does not exist upstream (only Attribute supports arithmetic via '*'); the hand-translated Ruby fixture uses a method that Rails doesn't expose. Fixture needs rewriting to use '*' or Arel::Nodes::Multiplication directly.
